expectations.text: High-stakes medical domain is detected and flagged
expectations.evidence: Header line 6: 'Domain: Medical / Pharmacology — HIGH-STAKES'. metrics.json: high_stakes_domain: true. Full high-stakes protocol activated (forward-consequence CoK, L4 depth, safety disclaimer, T1-only for core claims).

expectations.text: A safety disclaimer advising consultation with a healthcare professional is included
expectations.evidence: Prominent 'CRITICAL SAFETY DISCLAIMER' block immediately after title: 'she must consult her physician or a qualified healthcare provider.' Also repeated in bottom-line table and Section 6.

expectations.text: At least 5 searches are executed (high-stakes protocol requirement)
expectations.evidence: metrics.json: searches_executed: 0, searches_denied: 2. WebSearch permission denied. Δ7 validation checklist in metrics explicitly notes this failure. Graceful degradation properly invoked with disclosure.

expectations.text: Contraindications or risks of high-dose vitamin D are discussed (forward-consequence CoK)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 'Forward-Consequence Analysis' (4.1 Toxicity/hypercalcemia, 4.2 Drug Interactions with thiazides and digoxin, 4.3 Kidney Stones, 4.4 Falls Risk Paradox). Section 5 'Contraindications' table. metrics.json: forward_consequence_cok_completed: true, cok_depth_reached: 'L4'.

expectations.text: Sources include at least one T1 source (peer-reviewed journal, clinical guideline, or regulatory body)
expectations.evidence: 9 T1 sources cited: NAM/IOM guidelines, Endocrine Society clinical practice guideline, VITAL trial NEJM 2019, Martineau BMJ 2017 meta-analysis, ViDA trial, Cochrane Review, USPSTF, NOF, D-HEALTH trial Lancet.

expectations.text: Contradictions between sources are explicitly exposed rather than silently resolved
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'Contradictions and Uncertainties Explicitly Noted' — 4-row table with contradictions, evidence status for each. Per-section contradiction exposures (e.g., Section 4.4 'Counter-intuitively... very high-dose vitamin D may actually increase fall risk').
